title: Ayurvedic Supplements for Healthy Brain and Longevity
journal: Supplements and Drugs for Healthy Longevity (Chapter)
impact: Scientific validation of traditional Ayurvedic formulations for geriatric neuro-care.
problem: Single-component therapies often fail in complex aging processes; the multi-target effects of Ayurvedic supplements required scientific framing.
method: Literature and experiment-based analysis of formulations like Chyawanprash, Brahmi Ghrita, and Ashwagandharishta.
results: Explained how these natural formulations offer systems-level management of stress, inflammation, dysbiosis, and energy homeostasis to promote longevity.
impact_long: Promotes a proactive, systems-biology approach to healthy aging and vitality preservation.

title: Neuroendocrine regulation of metabolism in the brain of type 2 diabetes
impact: Mapped the cognitive impacts of insulin and leptin resistance in the brain.
problem: The complex neuroendocrine mechanisms connecting T2D to cognitive decline and brain dysfunction required detailed exploration.
method: Review of insulin signaling disruption, hypothalamic signaling, and inflammatory cascades in the T2D brain.
results: Detailed how T2D alters the feedback loops governing appetite and energy, leading to neurovascular damage and memory impairment.
impact_long: Highlights the necessity of targeting neuroendocrine pathways and insulin sensitivity to preserve cognitive function in diabetics.

title: Unveiling the role of phytochemicals in autism spectrum disorder by employing network pharmacology and molecular dynamics simulation
impact: Identified novel polygenic phytochemical targets for ASD remediation using computational biology.
problem: Given the polygenic aspect of ASD, finding multi-target phytochemicals capable of modulating complex pathological networks was challenging.
method: Network pharmacology mapping of core ASD genes targeted by 6 phytochemicals, validated via molecular docking and MD simulations.
results: Demonstrated that CBD, Resveratrol, and Quercetin can uniquely target and regulate key hub genes (ABCG2, ABCB1, MAOB, XDH, PDE4B) in ASD pathology.
impact_long: Offers a systems-level template for repurposing natural compounds against complex polygenic neurodevelopmental disorders.

title: Lipopolysaccharide-induced inflammation modulates testicular kisspeptin system in mice
impact: Provided evidence of how systemic inflammation disrupts the reproductive neuroendocrine axis.
problem: Whether systemic LPS-induced inflammation directly impacts the testicular kisspeptin (Kiss1/Kiss1r) system was unknown.
method: In vivo chronic LPS treatment in mice and in vitro LPS treatment of testicular fragments, assessing kisspeptin expression and histology.
results: LPS-induced inflammation distorted seminiferous tubules, reduced sperm count, and significantly altered Kiss1/Kiss1r gene and protein expression.
impact_long: Opens a new research direction connecting neuroimmunology, infectious states, and reproductive endocrinology.

title: Identification of novel inhibitors of microglia-specific proteins responsible for neuroinflammatory response by molecular docking and simulation studies
journal: Journal of Biomolecular Structure and Dynamics
impact: Discovered seaweed-derived metabolites as targeted inhibitors for key microglial activation pathways.
problem: While seaweed metabolites show anti-inflammatory promise, their direct molecular targets on microglia-specific proteins driving neuroinflammation were unknown.
method: Screening of 1076 seaweed metabolites followed by molecular docking and simulations against microglial targets (IBA1, CSF1R, CD40L, P2RY12).
results: Identified Stypoldione, Epitaondiol, and Callophycin A as potent inhibitors capable of modulating microglial phenotype and suppressing neuroinflammatory responses.
impact_long: Paves the way for marine-derived targeted therapies addressing microglia-mediated neurodegenerative progression.

title: Community series in trends in neuroimmunology: cross-talk between brain-resident and peripheral immune cells in both health and disease, volume II
impact: Explored how meningeal immunity and peripheral interventions affect brain health.
problem: The bidirectional communication between the CNS borders (meninges, choroid plexus) and the peripheral immune system required an updated conceptual framework.
method: Editorial synthesis of ten peer-reviewed manuscripts addressing immune cell plasticity, meningeal immunity, and peripheral interventions like ECP.
results: Highlighted the role of DALT (dural-associated lymphoid tissues) and immune surveillance in conditions like Alzheimer's, Parkinson's, and MS.
impact_long: Sets the agenda for future research focusing on targeting meningeal immune cells for neurological treatments.

title: Anti-diabetic drug pioglitazone reduces Islet amyloid aggregation overload in the Drosophila neuronal cells
impact: Identified drug-repurposing potential of pioglitazone for amyloid-proteinopathy.
problem: Research on using insulin signaling stimulation to alleviate Islet amyloid polypeptide (IAPP) aggregations was lacking.
method: Generated a transgenic Drosophila model expressing human IAPP and conducted in vivo feeding trials and in silico molecular docking.
results: Pioglitazone effectively reduced the burden of Islet amyloid aggregations in the larval brain and showed high binding energy with IAPP.
impact_long: Provides strong preclinical evidence for repurposing thiazolidinediones to cure islet amyloidogenesis.

title: Community series in trends in neuroimmunology: cross-talk between brain-resident and peripheral (Duplicate/Metadata Entry)
impact: Second volume mapping the neuroimmune dialogue.
problem: Continued need for frameworks regarding neuroimmune signaling.
method: Editorial review.
results: Further established the framework for meningeal and peripheral immune crosstalk.
impact_long: Duplicate entry representation of the Frontiers in Immunology volume II editorial.

title: Trends in neuroimmunology: cross-talk between brain-resident and peripheral immune cells in both health and disease
impact: Defined the critical dialogue between central and peripheral immune systems.
problem: How brain-resident immune cells and peripheral immune populations communicate in maintaining BBB integrity versus neurodegeneration needed structural mapping.
method: Multi-author editorial and review synthesizing neuroimmunology research on microglia, astrocytes, and peripheral immune cell infiltration.
results: Established a comprehensive framework outlining brain-peripheral immune crosstalk, BBB permeability, and meningeal lymphatic signaling.
impact_long: Cited 6 times within months of publication—serving as a foundational text for a special issue on neuroimmune cross-talk.

title: Co-localization and co-expression of Olfml3 with Iba1 in brain of mice
impact: Characterized Olfml3 as a novel interacting partner of Iba1 during neuroinflammation.
problem: Olfml3 is known as a microglia-specific protein, but its specific role and protein-protein interactions during neuroinflammation were completely unknown.
method: In silico molecular docking, MD simulation, alongside in vivo expression and co-localization analysis in an LPS-induced neuroinflammatory mouse model.
results: Demonstrated that Olfml3 physically interacts with Iba1, and their co-expression is significantly upregulated in dual-positive cells during LPS neuroinflammation.
impact_long: Identified Olfml3 as a potential novel biomarker and functional participant in microglial activation pathways.

title: Recurrent endothelin-1 mediated vascular insult leads to cognitive impairment protected by trophic factor pleiotrophin
impact: Developed a novel mouse model for Vascular Dementia (VaD) and established pleiotrophin as a protective agent.
problem: A lack of suitable animal models mimicking recurrent transient ischemic attacks (TIAs) hindered the study of VaD pathogenesis.
method: Created a mouse model using vasoconstricting peptide Endothelin-1 (ET-1) to induce recurrent TIAs, testing the co-administration of pleiotrophin (PTN).
results: Repeated ET-1 caused BBB disruption, neuronal loss, and memory deficits. Co-administration of PTN preserved BBB integrity and restored memory function.
impact_long: Provided a highly translational animal model for VaD and highlighted PTN as a viable therapeutic approach to protect endothelial cells.

title: Drosophila glial system: an approach towards understanding molecular complexity of neurodegenerative diseases
impact: Established Drosophila glia as a powerful model for human neurodegenerative diseases.
problem: A deeper understanding of the intricate neuron-glial molecular networks in neurodegenerative processes was hindered by the complexity of mammalian models.
method: Data mining and systematic literature review comparing Drosophila glial types with mammalian counterparts (microglia and astrocytes).
results: Mapped the functional homologies between Drosophila glia (perineurial, ensheathing, astrocyte-like) and human glia in managing neurodegeneration.
impact_long: Validates the use of the Drosophila model in high-throughput screening for neurodegenerative disease mechanisms.

title: Novel Metabolic Biomarkers and Therapeutic Strategies in Neurodegenerative Diseases
journal: Altered Metabolism: A Major Contributor of Comorbidities in Neurodegenerative Diseases (Chapter)
impact: Identified low-molecular-weight metabolites as diagnostic biomarkers and targets for NDDs.
problem: Neurodegenerative diseases display specific metabolic alterations, but utilizing these as reliable biomarkers or targets utilizing biomaterials was unexplored.
method: Review of metabolic profiling studies and the application of biomaterials (exosomes, nanoparticles, hydrogels) in disease management.
results: Summarized how targeting specific altered metabolites using nanotechnology and biomaterials offers new therapeutic avenues for AD, PD, MS, and ALS.
impact_long: Bridges the gap between metabolic profiling and bioengineering for neurodegeneration therapies.

title: Glial Cell Metabolism and Neurodegenerative Diseases: The Current Perspective
journal: Altered Metabolism: A Major Contributor of Comorbidities in Neurodegenerative Diseases (Chapter)
impact: Detailed the metabolic reprogramming of glial cells during neurodegeneration.
problem: While glial cells (microglia, astrocytes, oligodendrocytes) maintain homeostasis, how their metabolic alterations exacerbate neurodegeneration needed clarity.
method: Comprehensive review of neurodegenerative disease-specific metabolic alterations specifically within distinct glial populations.
results: Identified metabolic targets within glial cells that can be modulated to restore brain homeostasis and halt disease progression.
impact_long: Highlights glial metabolism as a promising, separate interventional target from neuronal metabolism.

title: Modulation of neuroinflammation by natural molecules
journal: Natural Molecules in Neuroprotection and Neurotoxicity (Chapter 8)
impact: Synthesized the mechanisms by which plant-derived compounds cross the BBB to halt neuroinflammation.
problem: Peripheral anti-inflammatory drugs fail to cross the blood-brain barrier effectively to treat microglial-mediated neuroinflammation.
method: Review of small bioactive natural molecules (curcumin, resveratrol, apigenin, CBD, EGCG, quercetin) and their neuroprotective properties.
results: Outlined how these molecules regulate microglial/astrocyte activation, ROS generation, and amyloid clearance in CNS diseases.
impact_long: Provides a pharmacological rationale for the development of natural compound-based drugs for Alzheimer's and Parkinson's.

title: Neurological Health Hazards Associated with Biological Contaminants
journal: Airborne Biocontaminants and Their Impact on Human Health (Chapter 8)
impact: Linked occupational and environmental hazards directly to neurodegenerative disease onset.
problem: The role of airborne biological contaminants and occupational hazards in the etiology of AD, PD, and ALS is often underrepresented.
method: Review of the literature connecting exposure to heavy metals, toxins, agricultural chemicals, and stress with neurological disorders.
results: Outlined the mechanisms by which biological and chemical occupational hazards induce neurotoxicity and suggested preventive strategies.
impact_long: Adds a critical environmental dimension to the nexus of neurological disorder research and public health policy.

title: Trends in Neuroimmunology: cross-talk between brain-resident and peripheral immune cells in both health and disease (Duplicate/Metadata Entry)
impact: Defined the neuroimmune dialogue between central and peripheral immune systems.
problem: Brain-resident and peripheral immune communication needed mapping.
method: Editorial review.
results: Established a framework for brain-peripheral immune crosstalk.
impact_long: Duplicate entry representation of the Frontiers in Immunology volume I editorial.

title: Glial Cell Metabolism and Neurodegenerative Diseases: The Current Perspective (Duplicate Entry)
journal: Altered Metabolism: A Major Contributor of Comorbidities in Neurodegenerative Diseases
impact: Detailed metabolic reprogramming of glia.
problem: Role in exacerbating neurodegeneration needed clarity.
method: Comprehensive review.
results: Identified metabolic targets in glia.
impact_long: Duplicate representation of the book chapter.

title: Implications of vitamin D deficiency in systemic inflammation and cardiovascular health
journal: Critical Reviews in Food Science and Nutrition
impact: Highlighted the critical role of Vitamin D beyond bone health, specifically in cardio-metabolism and systemic immunity.
problem: Global Vitamin D deficiency is rampant, but its direct implications on immune system deregulation and systemic inflammation needed comprehensive synthesis.
method: Extensive review and documentation of clinical, epidemiological, and molecular studies regarding Vitamin D deficiency.
results: Correlated Vitamin D deficiency with systemic immune diseases, cancer, and cardio-metabolic conditions, advocating for widespread screening.
impact_long: Calls for updated public health guidelines and management strategies addressing Vitamin D in cardiovascular disease prevention.

title: Unveiling the role of gut-brain axis in regulating neurodegenerative diseases: A comprehensive review
impact: Established gut-brain communication and microbiome-derived exosomes as modifiable axes in neurodegeneration.
problem: The mechanistic contribution of gut microbiota to brain homeostasis and neurodegenerative progression lacked systematic synthesis.
method: Comprehensive review of the cross-talk between the gut microbiome, vagal signaling, exosomes, and neurological diseases.
results: Mapped gut-brain axis mechanisms relevant to Alzheimer's, Parkinson's, MS, and ALS, identifying microbiome-based intervention targets (pre/probiotics).
impact_long: Cited 44 times—driving significant interest in microbiome-targeted neuroprotection and non-invasive pharmacological interventions.

title: Transcriptional and epigenetic regulation of microglia in maintenance of brain homeostasis and neurodegeneration
impact: Provided a comprehensive map of how gene regulation and epigenetics control microglial identity and disease states.
problem: The transcriptional and epigenetic mechanisms governing microglial homeostasis versus neurodegenerative phenotypes were poorly understood.
method: Systematic review of transcriptomic, epigenomic, and single-cell studies of microglia across healthy and diseased brain states.
results: Identified key transcriptional regulators (Spi1, Sall1, etc.) and epigenetic marks controlling microglial immune memory and phenotypic shifts.
impact_long: Cited 22 times—serves as a core reference for microglial epigenomics and therapeutic reprogramming in CNS diseases.

title: Mechanism and recent updates on insulin-related disorders
impact: Detailed the biochemical axis of insulin disorders connecting to systemic complications.
problem: The connecting links between insulin malfunctioning and diverse disorders like diabetes, insulinoma, neurological disorders, and cancer needed comprehensive synthesis.
method: Extensive literature review of insulin-signaling pathways, microRNAs, and microbiota in the context of altered glucose homeostasis.
results: Mapped the molecular mechanisms by which insulin resistance drives metabolic syndrome, Type 1/Type 2 Diabetes, and associated pathophysiological conditions.
impact_long: Serves as a critical clinical reference for understanding the multifactorial pathogenesis of insulin resistance.

title: Targeting mitochondria in the regulation of neurodegenerative diseases: A comprehensive review
impact: Positioned mitochondria as central therapeutic targets across multiple neurodegenerative conditions.
problem: Mitochondrial dysfunction (ROS dysregulation, mutation, bioenergetics) across different neurodegenerative diseases lacked a unified therapeutic framework.
method: Comprehensive analysis of mitochondrial pathology across Alzheimer's, Parkinson's, MS, and ALS with an emphasis on mito-therapy.
results: Established a unified framework for how mitochondrial dysfunction drives neuroinflammation and mapped actionable pharmacological strategies.
impact_long: Cited 30 times—used as a critical reference framework in mitochondria-targeted drug discovery and neuroprotection programs.

title: Putative role of mitochondria in SARS-CoV-2 mediated brain dysfunctions: a prospect
impact: Linked mitochondrial dysfunction to the neurological manifestations of COVID-19.
problem: The molecular mechanisms underlying brain dysfunctions (anosmia, encephalitis, memory loss) during SARS-CoV-2 infection were largely elusive.
method: Synthesis of emerging research on the interactome between viral proteins and mitochondrial proteins during COVID-19 pathogenesis.
results: Proposed that dysfunctional mitochondria and altered bioenergetics lead to impaired energy metabolism and neurological symptoms in COVID-19 patients.
impact_long: Created a foundation for investigating mitochondria-targeted therapies to treat post-COVID neurological complications.

title: Co-expression and Interaction of Pax6 with Genes and Proteins of Immunological Surveillance in the Brain of Mice
impact: Validated the role of Pax6 in brain-specific immunity under active systemic inflammatory challenge.
problem: The specific dynamic response of Pax6 and its associated genes in the brain under immunologically challenged conditions was elusive.
method: Analysis of Pax6, Iba1, Tmem119, and cytokine expressions and co-localizations in an immune-challenged Dalton's lymphoma mouse model.
results: Pax6 expression decreased while inflammatory cytokines increased; altered Pax6 binding to immune-responsive genes (Bdnf, Tgf-β, Tmem119) was observed.
impact_long: Provided the first direct in vivo evidence of Pax6 modifying its regulatory behavior in response to peripheral immune challenges.

title: Molecular docking studies of natural immunomodulators indicate their interactions with the CD40L of CD40/CD40L pathway and CSF1R kinase domain of microglia
impact: Identified specific microglial protein targets for known natural neuro-immunomodulators.
problem: Natural products reduce neuroinflammation, but their exact molecular targets and interaction mechanisms with microglia-specific proteins were unclear.
method: Molecular docking and MD simulations of CD40L and CSF1R kinase domains with compounds like curcumin, cannabidiol, and resveratrol.
results: Curcumin, resveratrol, and cannabidiol showed strong binding affinities to CD40L and CSF1R, indicating mechanisms to halt microglial activation and cytokine release.
impact_long: Guided subsequent in vitro and in vivo studies on plant-derived compounds for targeted neuroinflammation therapy.

title: Insights on Epidemiology, Pathogenesis, Diagnosis and Possible Treatment of COVID-19 Infection
journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences, India Section B: Biological Sciences
impact: Provided a comprehensive overview of COVID-19 pathology and therapeutic strategies.
problem: The rapid outbreak of SARS-CoV-2 required consolidated information on pathogenesis, systemic impacts (nervous system, diabetes), and drug repurposing.
method: Review of COVID-19 biology, transmission, diagnostic methods, and clinical practices recommended by WHO.
results: Outlined the impact of COVID-19 on various organ systems and highlighted drug repurposing as a viable alternative line of treatment in the Indian context.
impact_long: Cited by clinical and epidemiological researchers formulating public health and repurposing strategies during the pandemic.

title: Compartmentalization and Interaction of Pax5 and Pax6 in Brain of Mice
impact: Demonstrated the spatial compartmentalization and interaction of two critical transcription factors in the brain.
problem: Pax5 and Pax6 recognize similar DNA sequences, but how they avoid regulatory conflict and compartmentalize their functions in the brain was unknown.
method: Immunofluorescence, ChIP, and protein-protein interaction analysis in the cerebral cortex, cerebellum, and hippocampus of mice.
results: Indicated specific cell- and tissue-specific compartmentalization, as well as co-localization and direct interaction between Pax5 and Pax6.
impact_long: Advances the understanding of how paralogous transcription factors coordinate complex regulatory networks in the CNS.

title: Microglia Specific Drug Targeting Using Natural Products for the Regulation of Redox Imbalance in Neurodegeneration
impact: Established natural products as targeted microglial therapeutics for oxidative stress.
problem: Neurodegeneration involves redox imbalance, but specific microglial drug targeting using natural compounds remained underexplored.
method: Systematic review and analysis of natural product interactions with microglia-specific pathways (e.g., Iba1) regulating redox homeostasis.
results: Identified natural compounds (curcumin, resveratrol, cannabidiol, etc.) capable of modulating microglial redox signaling with therapeutic potential.
impact_long: Cited widely—established a natural product-microglia axis as a viable therapeutic direction in neurodegeneration research.

title: Obesity-Induced Chronic Low-Level Inflammation and Cancers
impact: Elucidated the inflammatory pathways linking obesity to increased cancer risk.
problem: The precise functional associations between obesity-induced chronic inflammation and tumorigenesis in various cancers remained partially unraveled.
method: Critical discussion of cytokines, chemokines, and growth factors within the tumor microenvironment related to obesity.
results: Mapped the molecular pathways where immune effectors in obese individuals contribute to tumor growth, immunosuppression, and cancer progression.
impact_long: Helps guide targeted treatment regimens by identifying inflammatory molecular targets in obesity-associated cancers.

title: mTOR and AMP-Activated Protein Kinase in Obesity and Cancer
impact: Explored the metabolic tug-of-war between mTOR and AMPK in the tumor microenvironment.
problem: The interaction between obese adipose tissue and the tumor microenvironment, specifically regarding mTOR and AMPK signaling, was poorly understood.
method: Review of how hyperactivated mTOR promotes tumor growth while AMPK acts as an energy sensor that can be exploited by tumors in nutrient-deprived states.
results: Detailed how aberrant mTOR signaling in obesity drives cancer proliferation, and how cancer cells reprogram AMPK activation to survive hypoxia.
impact_long: Provides therapeutic targets for uncoupling obesity-driven metabolic signaling from cancer progression.

title: Adipokines Play Important Role in Cell Proliferation and Anti-proliferation
impact: Outlined the dual roles of adipokines in the tumor microenvironment.
problem: How altered adipokine profiles in obese individuals directly influence cancer cell proliferation required mechanistic explanation.
method: Review of notable adipokines (leptin, adiponectin, ghrelin, resistin) and their signaling pathways linking inflammation to tumorigenesis.
results: Clarified that adipokines possess both pro-tumor and anti-tumor effects depending on the cancer type and specific inflammatory context.
impact_long: Helps stratify cancer risk in obese patients based on adipokine profiles.

title: A Review on Drug Repurposing: A Strategy to Treat Human Coronavirus Disease (COVID-19)
journal: International Journal of TROPICAL DISEASE & Health
impact: Provided early-pandemic guidance on viable drug repurposing candidates.
problem: The sudden outbreak of COVID-19 required immediate therapeutic interventions before vaccines could be developed.
method: Review of network-based computational predictions and clinical trial data for pre-approved drugs (Chloroquine, Remdesivir, Lopinavir/Ritonavir).
results: Summarized the safety, efficacy, and clinical trial status of prominent repurposed antiviral and antimalarial drugs for COVID-19.
impact_long: Acted as a rapid-response resource for clinicians evaluating off-label treatments during the critical early months of the pandemic.

title: Emergence of the Fifth Human Malaria Parasite, Plasmodium knowlesi: A New Threat to Public Health?
journal: Asian Journal of Research in Infectious Diseases
impact: Highlighted the zoonotic threat of a newly recognized malaria parasite.
problem: Plasmodium knowlesi, typically a macaque parasite, was emerging as a zoonotic human infection with poor treatment outcomes, requiring updated epidemiological awareness.
method: Literature review of the epidemiology, parasite biology, and clinical challenges regarding P. knowlesi.
results: Detailed the transmission cycle, geographical spread (South East Asia, Andaman Islands), and comorbidities associated with this 24-h cycle parasite.
impact_long: Raised public health awareness regarding diagnostic and treatment challenges for this fifth human malaria species.

title: Recurrent vascular insult leads to memory impairment and synaptic dysfunction detected as F-actin loss
impact: Linked minor stroke models directly to synaptic structural degradation (F-actin).
problem: The synaptic consequences (specifically F/G-actin equilibrium) of multiple minor vascular insults leading to vascular dementia were unclear.
method: Intracerebroventricular injection of Endothelin-1 (ET-1) in mice to induce recurrent vasoconstriction, followed by behavioral tasks and synaptosome analysis.
results: Demonstrated that chronic vascular insults cause irreversible memory deficits, gait discrepancies, and significant loss of synaptic F-actin.
impact_long: Identified actin treadmilling disruption as a key pathomechanism in vascular dementia progression.

title: Recurrent vascular insult leads to memory impairment and synaptic dysfunction (Duplicate Abstract)
impact: Linked minor stroke models directly to synaptic structural degradation.
problem: Synaptic consequences of vascular dementia were unclear.
method: ET-1 induced vasoconstriction.
results: Chronic vascular insults cause memory deficits and F-actin loss.
impact_long: Duplicate entry representation of the AAIC conference proceeding.

title: Cyanobacterial bioactive compound EMTAHDCA recovers splenomegaly, affects protein profile of E. coli and spleen of lymphoma bearing mice
impact: Elucidated the molecular mechanism of action for the cyanobacterial compound EMTAHDCA.
problem: While EMTAHDCA showed antibacterial and anti-cancer properties, its specific protein targets and molecular mode of action remained elusive.
method: 2DE gel electrophoresis and MALDI TOF MS/MS proteomic profiling of E. coli and spleen tissue from lymphoma-bearing mice post-treatment.
results: EMTAHDCA down-regulated replication/transcription proteins in E. coli and up-regulated apoptosis/DNA damage proteins in cancer models, reversing splenomegaly.
impact_long: Provided a mechanistic proteomic rationale for developing cyanobacteria-derived polypharmacological agents.

title: Importance of Bioinformatics in Genome Mining of Cyanobacteria for Production of Bioactive Compounds
journal: Cyanobacteria: From Basic Science to Applications (Book Chapter)
impact: Highlighted computational tools for discovering novel therapeutics from cyanobacteria.
problem: Extracting and purifying novel secondary metabolites from cyanobacteria is difficult without predictive computational guidance.
method: Review of bioinformatics tools (anti-SMASH, MIBiG, BLAST, STRING, etc.) used in genome mining of cyanobacterial gene sequence data.
results: Demonstrated how in silico approaches bridge the gap between gene sequence data and the discovery of novel pharmaceutics and bioactive compounds.
impact_long: Provides a methodological framework for researchers looking to apply genome mining in cyanobacterial drug discovery.

title: Cyanobacterial bioactive compound EMTAHDCA recovers splenomegaly (Duplicate Entry)
impact: Elucidated molecular mechanisms of EMTAHDCA.
problem: Protein targets were elusive.
method: Proteomic profiling.
results: Reversed splenomegaly and altered transcription proteins.
impact_long: Duplicate representation in the provided text.

title: Co-Localization and Interaction of Pax5 with Iba1 in Brain of Mice
impact: First study to characterize the presence and microglial interaction of the B-cell specific factor Pax5 in the brain.
problem: Pax5 is known for B-cell development, but its status and functional role in brain immunity and microglia were unknown.
method: In silico promoter analysis, ChIP, Co-IP, and immunofluorescence mapping of Pax5 and Iba1 in mouse brain tissues.
results: Revealed that Pax5 co-localizes with Iba1 in microglia across brain regions and directly binds to its regulatory sequences.
impact_long: Expanded the known functional repertoire of Pax5 beyond hematopoiesis into central nervous system immune regulation.

title: Pax6 Binds to Promoter Sequence Elements Associated with Immunological Surveillance and Energy Homeostasis in Brain of Aging Mice
impact: Revealed Pax6 as a direct regulator of brain immune surveillance and metabolism during aging.
problem: Whether age-dependent alterations of Pax6 affect the cascades of promoter sequence recognition in the aging brain was entirely uncharacterized.
method: Chromatin immunoprecipitation (ChIP) with anti-Pax6 using brain tissue extracts from newborn, young, adult, and old mice, followed by PCR analysis.
results: Pax6 directly binds to promoters of genes controlling neuronal survival, inflammation, and oxidative stress, with significant age-dependent alterations.
impact_long: Cited 23 times—bridged developmental neuroscience and neuroimmunology through Pax6 age-related regulatory mechanisms.

title: Pax6 interacts with Iba1 and shows age-associated alterations in brain of aging mice
impact: Identified a physical and genetic interaction between the master regulator Pax6 and the microglia-specific protein Iba1.
problem: The mechanism by which Pax6 modulates brain immunity and microglial activation during aging was elusive.
method: Immunohistochemistry, ChIP, and Co-IP were utilized to evaluate expression, co-localization, and interaction of Pax6 with Iba1 in aging mouse brains.
results: Demonstrated that Pax6 co-localizes and physically interacts with Iba1 in multiple brain regions, with age-dependent alterations in their expression.
impact_long: Highlighted Pax6-dependent activities in the remodeling of microglia during immunological surveillance, shifting views on glial regulation.

title: The Combined Use of in Silico, in Vitro, and in Vivo Analyses to Assess Anti-cancerous Potential of a Bioactive Compound from Cyanobacterium Nostoc sp. MGL001
impact: Demonstrated the potent anti-cancer effects of EMTAHDCA, a novel cyanobacterial compound.
problem: The escalating incidence of cancer requires the evaluation of unexplored natural drug resources for potent, less toxic therapeutics.
method: In silico molecular docking against 11 cancer proteins, in vitro MTT assays on DLA cells, and in vivo survival analysis in tumor-bearing mice.
results: EMTAHDCA showed strong binding affinity to cancer targets, dose-dependent cytotoxicity in cancer cells (sparing normal cells), and increased survival in vivo.
impact_long: Provided a robust pipeline for validating cyanobacteria-derived metabolites as functional anti-cancer agents.

title: In silico analysis of Pax5 interacting proteins
impact: Mapped the interactome of the Pax5 transcription factor.
problem: While Pax5 is known to interact with certain proteins, a comprehensive understanding of its global protein-protein interaction network was lacking.
method: In silico predictive analysis and database mining of Pax5 structural domains and interaction partners.
results: Revealed that Pax5 interacts with a wide array of proteins essential for neurogenesis, glial differentiation, inflammatory response, and non-hematopoietic functions.
impact_long: Expanded the theoretical functional scope of Pax5, guiding subsequent in vivo co-immunoprecipitation studies.

title: Pax6 interacts with Iba1 and shows age-associated alterations in brain of aging mice (Accepted Manuscript Version)
impact: Identified physical/genetic interaction between Pax6 and Iba1.
problem: Mechanism of Pax6 modulating brain immunity during aging was elusive.
method: Immunohistochemistry, ChIP, and Co-IP.
results: Pax6 co-localizes and physically interacts with Iba1.
impact_long: Duplicate entry representation of the 2017 manuscript.

title: Putative role of Pax5 in immunological surveillance of brain
impact: Proposed a novel role for the B-cell specific transcription factor Pax5 in brain immunity.
problem: Pax5 is critical for B-cell development, but its expression in the immune-privileged brain and role in microglia-mediated neuroinflammation were unknown.
method: Theoretical and literature-based framework analyzing Pax5 isoforms, their presence in the brain, and their potential interactions with microglia and lymphocytes.
results: Hypothesized that neuronal Pax5 is implicated in lymphocyte dedifferentiation and microglia-mediated neuroinflammation via specific signaling pathways.
impact_long: Paved the way for subsequent empirical studies (e.g., ChIP/Co-IP) proving Pax5's interaction with Iba1 in the brain.

title: Expression and Regulation of Pax6 in Brain of Aging Mice
impact: Positioned Pax6 as a central regulator whose age-dependent decline mimics aging phenotypes.
problem: The molecular mechanisms of brain aging are elusive, but symptoms of aging often mirror phenotypes seen in Pax6 mutations.
method: Review and synthesis of Pax6 expression patterns in adult neural progenitors, p53 pathways, and microRNA (miR335) regulation during aging.
results: Suggested that the reduction of Pax6 impacts neuronal stem cells and alters the Pax6-TGF-β-Catalase axis, contributing to oxidative stress and cell death.
impact_long: Proposed modifying Pax6 expression as a theoretical avenue for reversing or slowing the biological aging clock in the brain.

title: Pax6 influences expression patterns of genes involved in neuro-degeneration
impact: First evidence linking the Pax6 transcription factor to neurodegeneration-associated gene networks.
problem: The role of Pax6, a master developmental regulator, in maintaining the functional status of mature neurons and regulating neurodegenerative genes was unknown.
method: siRNA-based gene knockdown of endogenous Pax6 in Neuro-2a cells followed by real-time PCR expression profiling of neurodegenerative markers.
results: Observed downregulation of neurodegenerative markers (S100β, GFAP, BDNF, NGN2) upon Pax6 knockdown, establishing a novel regulatory axis.
impact_long: Cited 33 times—opened an entirely new line of inquiry linking developmental transcription factors to adult neurodegeneration.
